steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage describes harsh outdoor conditions (rain, snow, heat, humidity) where the sculpture is “exposed,” then immediately states it “remains in good condition.” The contradiction: the conditions that would normally damage sculpture don't damage this one.

steps.title: Select the transition
steps.content: ”That said” bridges the contrast: despite harsh conditions, the sculpture endures. The other choices misfire: “In addition” adds more harsh details; “On the contrary” awkwardly negates the stated conditions; “As a result” reverses causation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
